topic: reflecting on the year — private practice was the right call

turns.content: weird mood today. sat in the truck after my last call and just sort of thought about things for a bit. not in a bad way. just reflecting I suppose.
That doesn't sound like a bad thing at all. Sometimes you need a moment to take stock. What were you reflecting on?

just how things have gone this past while. moving to private practice was a big decision and I spent ages going back and forth on it. but sitting there today after a good call on a nice farm I thought aye. this was right.
That's a good feeling -- making a big career change and then having the quiet confirmation that it was the right one. What made today's call particularly good?

nothing dramatic. just a routine fertility check on some heifers. farmer was sound, weather was decent for once, cows behaved themselves. I was done by three and drove home with the window down. thats it. thats the whole thing. and it was enough.
There's a lot to be said for a day where everything just... works. Cooperative cows, decent weather, done at a reasonable hour. Those days probably felt rarer before the switch?

aye much rarer. I was stretched so thin before. covering too much ground, always behind, always knackered. now the caseload is manageable and I actually have time to do the job properly. which sounds basic but it makes a massive difference when youre up to your elbows in a cow.
Being able to do your job properly without constantly rushing sounds like it would make a huge difference to both outcomes and your wellbeing. Do you feel like the quality of your work has improved?

prob aye. not that it was bad before but when youre tired and rushed you cut corners without meaning to. now I can take my time, talk to the farmer properly, actually think about what im seeing instead of just reacting. the crohns is better managed too when im not running on four hours sleep.

That all sounds really positive. Less stress, better sleep, more time to be thorough -- and managing a chronic condition on top of a demanding job is much harder when you're burnt out. Sounds like the switch helped on multiple fronts.

aye. I dont want to make it sound like everything is perfect cos it isnt. still get up at daft oclock, still stand in the rain, still deal with animals that would rather kick me than cooperate. but the overall shape of things is better. thats all you can ask for really.
That's a really grounded way to look at it. Not perfect, but better. And you're doing work you clearly care about, which counts for a lot.
aye. dont tell anyone I said any of this though. ive got a reputation for being miserable and I intend to maintain it.
Your secret is safe with me. As far as anyone knows, you're deeply unhappy and the cows are terrible. Business as usual.
perfect. right thats enough feelings for one calendar year. back to normal tomorrow. cheers.
Ha, annual feelings quota met. Take care, Callum. Glad things are going well.
